title: Microsoft Graph API — Pagination, $batch, $filter, $select, Error Handling

confirmed_facts.fact: Pagination: Graph returns @odata.nextLink in the response body when more pages exist. Follow it with GET (no extra params — the URL includes them).
$top controls page size. Default varies by endpoint (typically 10-100). Max is endpoint-specific: /users max 999, /messages max 1000, /events max 50.
$select reduces payload: GET /users?$select=id,displayName,mail — only returns requested fields. Always use $select for large collections.
$filter supports: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, startsWith(), endsWith(), contains() (limited). Example: $filter=displayName eq 'John'
$count=true adds @odata.count to the response. Requires ConsistencyLevel: eventual header for directory objects (users, groups, apps).
Advanced queries on directory objects require BOTH ConsistencyLevel: eventual header AND $count=true — omitting either silently returns incomplete results.
$batch endpoint: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$batch with body {requests: [{id, method, url, headers?, body?}]}. Max 20 requests per batch.
Batch responses: {responses: [{id, status, headers, body}]} — status is per-request (some may be 200 while others are 429). Always check each response status.

Graph error format: {error: {code, message, innerError: {date, request-id, client-request-id}}}. Common codes: InvalidAuthenticationToken (401), Authorization_RequestDenied (403), Request_ResourceNotFound (404), activityLimitReached (429).
Throttling: Graph returns 429 with Retry-After header (seconds). Per-app limits vary by endpoint. Typical: 10,000 requests per 10 minutes per app per tenant.
$orderby sorts results: $orderby=displayName asc. Not all fields are sortable — unsortable fields return 400.
$expand retrieves related entities inline: GET /users/{id}?$expand=memberOf. Limit: max 20 expanded relationships.

implementation_notes:
Full pagination with all pages:
```python
def graph_paginate(session, url, params=None):
    results = []
    while url:
        resp = session.get(url, params=params, timeout=(10, 30))
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        results.extend(data.get('value', []))
        url = data.get('@odata.nextLink')
        params = None  # nextLink includes all query params
    return results
```
caveats: $skip is NOT supported on most Graph endpoints — use @odata.nextLink pagination instead
contains() filter only works on specific string fields (mail, displayName) and requires ConsistencyLevel: eventual on directory objects
$batch requests are NOT atomic — each sub-request succeeds or fails independently
Some endpoints (e.g., /security/alerts) use different pagination ($skipToken) — check the specific endpoint docs
Graph v1.0 and beta may have different filter/sort capabilities for the same resource
